retrieved.text:
Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Some courses have a limited number of participants.
- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)
- Either through the [point allocation system](#c600152), the [preference system](#c600156) or the [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/).
- Students on the [waiting list](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273323) will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places.

- Can I attend courses, even if I do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate?

  Yes. You can be admitted to a non-degree programme to attend individual courses if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. You do not have to fulfil any special admission requirements.
- How much does a non-degree programme cost?

  In a non-degree programme you have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- Which courses can I attend?

  Please inquire at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme that offers the courses you are interested in. retrieved.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. Your performance continues to count. - What happens if I do not take a certain number of exams? If you do not take a certain number of exams, it does not have any consequences for you personally in terms of study law. Students can have various reasons why they temporarily take fewer exams: gainful employment, family duties and care obligations. - If you are not able to take exams for a certain time due to personal reasons, you can apply for a [leave of absence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/leave-of-absence/). - Also students enrolled in more than one degree programme often take fewer exams. If you no longer actively study one or several of your degree programmes, you can [close the relevant degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programmes/). - Please also notify the University if you [discontinue exams or courses for an important reason](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/). This gives other students the opportunity to take a spot on the course or in the exam. - Which support measures help me to be ‘prüfungsaktiv’ (take a certain number of exams)? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. retrieved.text:
- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme also count for the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively in the non-degree programme, you are permanently excluded from the degree programme and cannot be admitted to this programme at the University of Vienna.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- You are not admitted to a specific degree programme, but to attending individual courses ("Besuch einzelner Lehrveranstaltungen").
- The admission to a non-degree programme is possibile until the end of the admission period. However, the registration deadlines for courses end before the beginning of the semester in some cases. Therefore, it is possible that you miss these deadlines.
- Students admitted to a degree programme are favoured in the allocation of course places. If you have any questions regarding the registration for courses and examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the courses are offered.
- You are only allowed to take individual examinations/courses and must comply with the curriuclum's registration requirements. Furthermore, you are not allowed to take any public defenses. Graduating is not possible.
- There might be restrictions/limitations when it comes to health insurance, residence permit and financial assistance. Please make an inquiry at the responsible authority about the consequences of your non-degree programme up front.

reranked.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
reranked.text:
Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Some courses have a limited number of participants.
- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)
- Either through the [point allocation system](#c600152), the [preference system](#c600156) or the [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/).
- Students on the [waiting list](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273323) will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places.

heading_path: Prüfungsaktiv – examination activity – what does this mean?
reranked.text: exams easier to ensure that students take a certain number of exams?esenkt und die Prüfungen leichter? No. The performance requirements are not changed at all. Your performance continues to count. - What happens if I do not take a certain number of exams? If you do not take a certain number of exams, it does not have any consequences for you personally in terms of study law. Students can have various reasons why they temporarily take fewer exams: gainful employment, family duties and care obligations. - If you are not able to take exams for a certain time due to personal reasons, you can apply for a [leave of absence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/leave-of-absence/). - Also students enrolled in more than one degree programme often take fewer exams. If you no longer actively study one or several of your degree programmes, you can [close the relevant degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programmes/). - Please also notify the University if you [discontinue exams or courses for an important reason](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/). This gives other students the opportunity to take a spot on the course or in the exam. - Which support measures help me to be ‘prüfungsaktiv’ (take a certain number of exams)? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/)

reranked.title: Attending courses out of interest
reranked.text:
- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme also count for the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively in the non-degree programme, you are permanently excluded from the degree programme and cannot be admitted to this programme at the University of Vienna.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- You are not admitted to a specific degree programme, but to attending individual courses ("Besuch einzelner Lehrveranstaltungen").
- The admission to a non-degree programme is possibile until the end of the admission period. However, the registration deadlines for courses end before the beginning of the semester in some cases. Therefore, it is possible that you miss these deadlines.
- Students admitted to a degree programme are favoured in the allocation of course places. If you have any questions regarding the registration for courses and examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the courses are offered.
- You are only allowed to take individual examinations/courses and must comply with the curriuclum's registration requirements. Furthermore, you are not allowed to take any public defenses. Graduating is not possible.
- There might be restrictions/limitations when it comes to health insurance, residence permit and financial assistance. Please make an inquiry at the responsible authority about the consequences of your non-degree programme up front.

selected.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
selected.text:
Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Some courses have a limited number of participants.
- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)
- Either through the [point allocation system](#c600152), the [preference system](#c600156) or the [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/).
- Students on the [waiting list](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273323) will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places.
